message:
fix(web): handle ambiguous bases correctly in anomaly worker

Problem:
- The entropy calculation divided by seq.length which includes N and
  other non-ACGT characters, skewing entropy values
- The GC content calculation used regex match which is inefficient and
  also divided by total window length including ambiguous bases
- The preprocessing step stripped all non-ACGT characters, changing
  positions and breaking coordinate alignment with the original sequence

Fixes:

Entropy calculation:
- Count only A, C, G, T bases for the total denominator
- Return 0 immediately if no valid bases (avoids division by zero)
- This gives accurate Shannon entropy for windows with ambiguous bases

GC content calculation:
- Replace regex-based counting with explicit character loop
- Track validBases (A+C+G+T count) and gcBases (G+C count) separately
- Divide gcBases by validBases instead of total window length
- Return 0 if no valid bases in window

Sequence preprocessing:
- Keep ambiguous bases (N) in the sequence to preserve positions
- Only convert to uppercase, don't strip characters
- Downstream functions now handle N correctly by excluding from counts

This ensures anomaly detection produces accurate metrics for real phage
genomes which commonly have ambiguous base calls in sequencing data.

Refs: phage_explorer-c45ec4a (related fix in core anomaly scanner)
